title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — June 2026 (v3.1 EIA-Validated Methodology)
methodology: Two-stage aggregation using exact BLS 2024-2025 relative importance weights. Stage 1: component-level forecasts. Stage 2: weighted aggregation. Verification: Food+Energy+Core=100.000% and Shelter+CoreGoods+CoreServices=80.557%. Energy components validated with AAA retail gasoline ($3.884/gal Jul 10) and EIA retail ($3.777/gal Jul 6). WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%). Backtest: 15 months weight accuracy MAE 0.035% core, 0.146% headline.
summary: June CPI headline +0.1% m/m (consensus +0.1–0.2%) on energy deflation from gasoline collapse. Core +0.2% m/m (consensus +0.2–0.3%). AAA retail gasoline $3.884/gal Jul 10 vs May avg ~$4.32 (-10%). ISM Manufacturing Prices cooling but Services Prices sticky. FOMC Jun 16-17 hawkish hold; dot plot 3.8% = one hike in 2026. 10Y yield 4.56% caps duration rally. Warsh hawkishness limits soft-print market reaction.
baseCase: Below-consensus headline CPI on energy deflation; core stable at +0.2% with shelter deceleration offsetting sticky services. FOMC hawkish hold caps rally.

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: AAA retail gasoline $3.884/gal Jul 10 vs May avg ~$4.32 (-10%). Energy CPI deflation ≈ -1.4% m/m composite. Exact BLS weights: Food 13.25% + Energy 6.19% + Core 80.56%. Core stable (+0.2%) with shelter cooling (0.25%) but services sticky (0.35%). 10Y yield 4.56% = key regime line. FOMC dot plot 3.8% median = one hike in 2026. Warsh hawkish hold limits soft-print market reaction.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.1% m/m, Core +0.2% m/m — gasoline decline dominates headline; core stable with shelter deceleration offsetting sticky services
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields fade to 4.45–4.50%, DXY 99.5–100.5. Tech (QQQ) rallies on rate-cut hope revival. Airlines (UAL) and homebuilders (DHI) benefit from energy/mortgage relief. Energy sector (XLE) underperforms. TLT duration play viable.
scenarios.invalidation: Core services re-accelerate above +0.35% m/m; ISM Services Prices exceed 75; Iran ceasefire collapses and oil spikes.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.3% m/m, Core +0.3% m/m — core PPI passthrough finally hits services; gasoline only partially declines
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields spike 4.60%+, DXY 101+. Tech selloff (NVDA, MSFT). Gold (GLD) squeeze. USD long. SPY/QQQ PUT setups. XLE short-cover rally on oil.
scenarios.invalidation: Hot print driven by one-off transportation component; core goods actually deflationary; PPI passthrough delayed to August.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% m/m, Core +0.15% m/m — oil collapse fully captured, DXY softens, shelter decelerates faster than expected
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y yields drop 4.40–4.45%, DXY 99–100. Aggressive risk-on: QQQ +2–3%, UAL +10%, DHI +5%. TLT strong. XLE -5%. Mortgage REITs rally.
scenarios.invalidation: Market already priced benign outcome; FOMC hawkish tone prevents rally; Warsh 'one data point' rhetoric.

setups.bias: Long duration on cooler or inline core
setups.trigger: Core CPI <= +0.2% and 10Y yield fails 4.55%; DXY below 100.5
setups.invalidation: Core services above +0.30% with ISM Prices >75; 10Y yield breaks 4.65%.

setups.bias: Long on soft headline — rate-cut pricing revived
setups.invalidation: Hot core with 10Y yield above 4.65%; DXY above 101.5.

setups.bias: Long — jet fuel collapse + summer demand intact
setups.trigger: AAA gasoline <$3.90 and CPI headline soft; summer bookings strong
setups.invalidation: Iran deal collapses, WTI above $80, or travel demand disappoints.

setups.bias: Long — mortgage rate relief on soft CPI + 10Y fade
setups.trigger: 10Y yield below 4.50% and core CPI inline or cooler
setups.invalidation: 10Y yields spike above 4.65% on hot CPI or PPI passthrough.

setups.bias: Short — oil supply glut, Iran ceasefire holding, demand weak
setups.trigger: WTI below $70 and Hormuz traffic normalizes; API inventories build
setups.invalidation: Iran ceasefire collapses, Israel attacks, or OPEC+ emergency cuts.

setups.bias: Long hedge — Fed uncertainty + Warsh unpredictability
setups.trigger: DXY below 100.0 and real yields compress; geopolitical flare-up
setups.invalidation: DXY above 101.5 with 10Y yields >4.70%; strong risk-on kills safe-haven bid.

watchItems.label: Fed path / Warsh
watchItems.note: FOMC Jun 16-17 hawkish hold. Warsh first meeting. 9/18 policymakers expect hike. PCE forecasts revised up to 3.6% headline, 3.3% core. Easing bias REMOVED from statement.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST
watchItems.note: Key regime line. Below 4.45% = growth rally. Above 4.65% = risk-off. Oil pulling down, FOMC + PPI pushing up.

watchItems.label: AAA Retail Gasoline
watchItems.note: AAA National Average. Down from $4.191 month ago (-7.3%). Down from ~$4.32 May avg. EIA retail $3.777 Jul 6. WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23 failed).

watchItems.label: ISM Prices Paid
watchItems.note: Manufacturing prices cooling from 82.1 peak but still elevated. Services prices sticky. Watch Jun Services print (due early July).

watchItems.label: DXY
watchItems.note: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets PPI passthrough. YTD +2.70%.

watchItems.label: Manheim Used Vehicle Index
watchItems.note: Used car prices firm but seasonal depreciation starting. EV index +11.9% y/y on gas price sensitivity. Full Jun release Jul 8.

watchItems.label: WTI Crude
watchItems.note: Down ~25% past month. Iran ceasefire + OPEC+ unwinding cuts. War premium evaporating. Directional only — NOT used for gasoline CPI forecast.

watchItems.label: VIX
watchItems.note: Pre-CPI normalization. Low VIX = complacency risk. CPI day could see 19–24 spike on surprise.

keyDrivers: Oil/gasoline collapse: AAA retail gasoline $3.884/gal Jul 10 vs May avg ~$4.32 (-10%). Energy CPI deflation ≈ -1.4% m/m composite. EIA retail/AAA is primary source; WTI futures NOT used for gasoline CPI per backtest (R²=0.23, MAE=8.1%).

FOMC hawkish hold: Warsh's first meeting Jun 16-17. Statement stripped easing bias. Dot plot 3.8% median = one hike in 2026. 9/18 participants pencil hike. Core PCE forecast 3.3%. Limits soft-CPI market reaction.

Shelter deceleration: Zillow/Apartment List leading indicators slowing. OER normalizing to ~0.25% m/m. Rent of primary residence ~2.5% y/y. But BLS 6-12 month lag means full deceleration not yet priced.
DXY ~100: Strong dollar = import deflation on core goods. Partially offsets any PPI passthrough. Core goods may see mild deflation (-0.1% m/m).
ISM prices divergence: Manufacturing prices cooling = goods disinflation. Services prices sticky = core services risk. Transportation/warehousing key wildcard.
Core PPI passthrough watch: Prior hot PPI prints feed into core CPI with 1-3 month lag. If passthrough accelerates in Jul/Aug, core undershoot narrative breaks.
risks: Core PPI passthrough: Hot PPI feeds into core CPI with 1-3 month lag. If services passthrough materializes in July, core surprise to upside.
Iran ceasefire collapse: Nuclear talks in Doha fragile. Israel/Lebanon tensions, US Congress opposition. WTI $80+ = CPI revision to hotter.
FOMC hawkish limits reaction: Market may 'sell the rally' even on soft CPI. Warsh 'price stability' rhetoric + missing dot = unpredictable Fed. One hike in 2026 priced.
ISM Services Prices re-acceleration: Jun print (due early July) if >75 = core services sticky confirmation.
Demand weakness: Global recession fears rising. If oil and risk assets both fall, soft CPI = bad news for equities (stagflation fear).
Shelter lag uncertainty: Zillow/Apartment List slowing but BLS 6-12 month lag. Faster deceleration = core downside surprise; slower = core sticky.
